quotes.text: Moab, which was named after the firstborn son of Lot's eldest daughter (Gen. 19:37), was the nation that bordered the Dead Sea on the east. The prophet warns the Moabites that they will be destroyed, despite the fact that they are prosperous in trading and commerce. The listing of sixteen cities and places in this section, which are located in both the northern and southern regions of Moab, indicates that all of Moab will be affected by the devastation.

notes: The inhabitants of Moab go to their temples (Hebrew Byth, "temple") and high places to mourn and lament because of the destruction. It was customary for males to demonstrate their sorrow by shaving their heads and beards (22:12; Micah 1:16). The once proud inhabitants of Moab are now reduced to humility and shame.
In the ancient Middle East, extreme mourning was done in public places so that all could see.
soldiers of Moab shall cry out [Even the hardened men of war will feel sorrow and will cry out]
heifer of three years old [is "in her strength," or full of vigor. Zoar was strong but soon would be
